articles.headline: Romania great Gheorghe Hagi returns to coach national team
articles.description: Romania great Gheorghe Hagi has been hired as national team coach for the second time. He was appointed to replace Mircea Lucescu, who died two weeks ago.

images.name: Gheorghe Hagi Romania coach_260421 [1024x576]
images.caption: Gheorghe Hagi agreed to coach the Romania national team for the second time in his career following a brief stint in 2001.
images.alt: Gheorghe Hagi agreed to coach the Romania national team for the second time in his career following a brief stint in 2001.

articles.headline: Rosenior calls for behaviour improvement after Chelsea's ninth red card
articles.description: Liam Rosenior describes Chelsea's indiscipline this season as a "learning experience" following Pedro Neto's red card vs. Arsenal and looks ahead to visiting Aston Villa.

images.name: Rosenior calls for behaviour improvement after Chelsea's ninth red card
images.caption: Liam Rosenior describes Chelsea's indiscipline this season as a "learning experience" following Pedro Neto's red card vs. Arsenal and looks ahead to visiting Aston Villa.

articles.headline: Ireland to fulfil Nations League games with Israel despite call for ban
articles.description: The Republic of Ireland said they will fulfil their Nations League fixtures against Israel in the autumn despite calling for the country to be banned by UEFA last year.

images.name: Ireland football logo [1296x729]
images.caption: The Irish football federation will call on UEFA to ban Israel.
images.alt: he official badge of the Republic of Ireland national football team on a shirt during the FIFA Women's World Cup Australia & New Zealand 2023 Group F match between France and Jamaica at Sydney Football Stadium on July 23, 2023 in Sydney, Australia

articles.headline: Nations League draw: England get Spain, Croatia, Czechia
articles.description: England face a rematch with their Euro 2024 final conquerors Spain in the 2026-27 Nations League.

images.name: England vs Spain Euro 2024 final [1296x729]
images.caption: Jude Bellingham and England were beaten by Spain the final of Euro 2024.
images.alt: Jude Bellingham of England in action during the UEFA EURO 2024 final match between Spain and England at Olympiastadion on July 14, 2024 in Berlin, Germany.

images.name: Did Thomas Tuchel deserve a contract extension with England?
images.caption: ESPN's Nedum Onuoha and Julien Laurens react to Thomas Tuchel signing a new contract extension with England until 2028.

articles.headline: Will Sesko's brace vs. Burnley kick-start his Man United career?
articles.description: Stewart Robson talks about Benjamin Sesko's performance after scoring two goals in Manchester United 2-2 draw with Burnley.

images.name: Will Sesko's brace vs. Burnley kick-start his Man United career?
images.caption: Stewart Robson talks about Benjamin Sesko's performance after scoring two goals in Manchester United 2-2 draw with Burnley.

articles.headline: Kylian Mbappe's injury 'not good news' for Real Madrid's schedule
articles.description: Gab Marcotti talks about Kylian Mbappe's recent knee injury that will have him out for at least three weeks for Real Madrid.

images.name: Kylian Mbappe's injury 'not good news' for Real Madrid's schedule

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal! Gibraltar 1, Liechtenstein 0. Liam Walker (Gibraltar) left footed shot from a difficult angle and long range on the right to the top left corner following a corner.
Sandro Wieser (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Fabio Notaro (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
First Half ends, Gibraltar 1, Liechtenstein 0.
Jayce Olivero (Gibraltar) is shown the yellow card.
Goal! Gibraltar 1, Liechtenstein 1. Ferhat Saglam (Liechtenstein) right footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Marcel Büchel.
Marcel Büchel (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card.
Graeme Torrilla (Gibraltar) is shown the yellow card.
Jaiden Bartolo (Gibraltar) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Gibraltar. Jaiden Bartolo replaces Tjay De Barr because of an injury.
Kenny Kindle (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Ethan Britto (Gibraltar) is shown the yellow card.
Livio Meier (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Gibraltar. Julian Valarino replaces Ethan Jolley.
Substitution, Liechtenstein. Marco Marxer replaces Kenny Kindle.
Substitution, Liechtenstein. Andrin Netzer replaces Fabio Notaro.
Aron Sele (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card.
Penalty saved. Liam Walker (Gibraltar) left footed shot saved in the bottom right corner.
Substitution, Liechtenstein. Fabio Wolfinger replaces Marcel Büchel.
Substitution, Liechtenstein. Martin Marxer replaces Maximilian Göppel.
Andrin Netzer (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Liechtenstein. Simon Lüchinger replaces Livio Meier.
Louie Annesley (Gibraltar) is shown the yellow card.
Dayle Coleing (Gibraltar) is shown the yellow card.
Lorenzo Lo Russo (Liechtenstein) is shown the yellow card.
Goal! Gibraltar 2, Liechtenstein 1. James Scanlon (Gibraltar) header from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Julian Valarino with a cross.
Joseph Chipolina (Gibraltar) is shown the red card.
Substitution, Gibraltar. Lee Casciaro replaces Liam Walker.
Goal! Gibraltar 2, Liechtenstein 2. Nicolas Hasler (Liechtenstein) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the top left corner.
Second Half ends, Gibraltar 2, Liechtenstein 2.
